Contact Me

  • Tweet

Do you have a problem and want to see if Sir Edward can help?

If so, please get in touch by email: [email protected]

Or you can write by post to: Sir Edward Leigh MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA.

Please be sure to include your name and postal address (so we can confirm you are a constituent) and, if possible, a telephone number or email address in case have any further questions regarding your case.

If you are writing as part of a campaign or on a political issue, please check to see if he has issued a response on the subject in question.

If a response has been posted, he will not normally send an individual response. Otherwise, Sir Edward will normally reply by regular post.

To make a surgery appoint to see Sir Edward, please leave a voicemail with your name and telephone number with Jessie Milne, who organises Sir Edward's surgery appointments. She may be contacted by telephone on 07587147116. Note: This number is for constituents requesting a surgery appointment only. Press inquiries should not leave a message on it but contact Sir Edward via email: [email protected]

Sir Edward Leigh will normally reply to correspondence from constituents via regular post.

Please note, however, that a strict parliamentary convention prevents him from making any public statement on certain bills when he has chaired either a Bill Committee or the Committee of the Whole House relating to that bill. This is to ensure the impartiality of parliamentary procedure.

He is also unable to take on the issues or cases of the constituents of other Members of Parliament.

The office of Sir Edward Leigh MP can be contacted by telephone on 02072196480, but if you are looking for help we will need you to email or write by regular post so we have a written record of your inquiry. Email is the quickest and most efficient way for your case to be acted upon swiftly.

Sir Edward Leigh MP Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency
